meta_title: For Founders: Antigravity project template to Solve can't finish projects (Intermediate Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to Antigravity project template and overcome can't finish projects. A Intermediate-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Antigravity Project Template: Finish Your SaaS MVP Faster
content.seo_description: Stop stalling on your SaaS. Use the Antigravity project template and Claude Code to bypass the 'can't finish' trap and launch your MVP with expert speed.
content.answer_block: The 'can't finish' trap stems from architectural drift and scope creep. By implementing an Antigravity project template, you enforce a strict, modular structure that automates boilerplate and forces MVP constraints. Combine this with Claude Code to execute tasks iteratively, ensuring your development cycle remains focused, rapid, and shipping-ready.
content.intro:
The graveyard of abandoned SaaS projects is littered with founders who started with fire but ended in feature-creep purgatory. You have the vision, but the technical friction of managing state, schema, and deployment eventually suffocates your momentum.

It is time to stop coding in the dark. The Antigravity project template acts as a structural exoskeleton, providing the rigid scaffolding required to turn a fragile idea into a hardened, production-ready application. When you pair this with the autonomous precision of Claude Code, you transform from a struggling developer into a high-velocity product engineer.

steps.title: Define the Atomic MVP Scope
steps.description: Strip your project down to the one core feature that delivers value. We use the template to enforce a clean separation of concerns.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Initialize an Antigravity project structure focused on a single-tenant SaaS MVP. Include essential auth, database schema, and API route boilerplate only.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Initialize project structure with Antigravity boilerplate'

steps.title: Automate Database Schema Modeling
steps.description: Use the template to generate type-safe models, preventing the common trap of mid-project database refactoring.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate Prisma schema definitions for a user-centric SaaS model including subscription status and basic entity relations.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Generate and sync database schema using Antigravity templates'

steps.title: Implement Modular API Handlers
steps.description: Standardize your endpoint structure to ensure that adding features doesn't create technical debt.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a modular API handler pattern that integrates with the Antigravity middleware stack for authentication.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Bootstrap API route handlers based on Antigravity standards'

steps.title: Configure the Frontend-Backend Bridge
steps.description: Establish a robust communication layer so the UI reflects the backend state without manual synchronization.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Setup type-safe API client generation using the Antigravity project bridge for seamless frontend integration.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Generate frontend API client from backend schema'

steps.title: Finalize Deployment Readiness
steps.description: Ensure the project is ready for CI/CD, eliminating the 'it works on my machine' barrier to launch.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure production-ready deployment settings and environment variable validation within the Antigravity template.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Configure production environment and deployment pipeline'

pro_tips: Treat your template as a product: Version control your Antigravity boilerplate and iterate on it every time you launch a new project.
Implement 'Feature Flags' from Day 1 to allow shipping incomplete code without breaking the production user experience.
Use Claude Code to perform automated code reviews on your own PRs to catch architectural drift before it becomes a bottleneck.
Limit your MVP session to 4-hour sprints; if a feature takes longer, it is too complex for an MVP—cut it.
Never manually edit boilerplate generated by the template; always update the generator logic to maintain long-term consistency.
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is scaffolded, push your repository to GitHub, configure your Vercel/Fly.io deployment pipeline, and focus exclusively on acquiring your first 10 paying users.
content.conversion_block: Stop building in circles and start shipping revenue-generating software. Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
